metadata.summary: Curriculum alignment to 2026 PMP Exam Content Outline. Shifts Business Environment 8% → 26%, emphasizing value delivery, governance, sustainability, and AI-in-PM.

content.title: PM Certification Alignment 2026
content.summary: Curriculum and content alignment to the 2026 PMP Exam Content Outline (ECO). The 2026 exam shifts Business Environment from 8% to 26% of exam weight, requiring curriculum realignment and explicit coverage of value delivery, sustainability, and AI-in-PM themes.

why.p: Most PM curricula and prep materials were built for the pre-2026 weighting (People 42% / Process 50% / Business Environment 8%). Candidates and organisations using legacy materials will under-invest in Business Environment topics now worth 26% of the exam. A crosswalk against the ECO identifies exactly which topics are under-covered and by how much.

list.item: Auditing an existing PM curriculum against the 2026 PMP ECO domain weights
Building a corporate "PMP-ready" path for candidates who hold PMP6 or PRINCE2
Generating tailored study tracks that weight under-covered Business Environment themes
Aligning internal PM methodology libraries to the new sustainability and AI-in-PM topics
Tagging existing content against ECO domain/task IDs for coverage tracking
General project execution work — certification alignment is academic; use delivery methodologies directly
Candidates whose exam date falls before the 2026 ECO version takes effect (check PMI.org for current form version)
Non-PMI certifications (PRINCE2 2025, IPMA, ISO 21502 conformity) with their own content outlines
PMP renewal (CCR) planning — that is a separate process from initial exam preparation

section.name: Agentic Workflow
section.p: The agent is a curriculum aligner and gap analyst.

list.item: (1) Ingest: read the 2026 ECO PDF, parse tasks per domain.
(2) Crosswalk: tag existing content (training modules, methodology pages, internal playbooks) against ECO tasks.
(3) Gap report: list ECO tasks with no content; rank by exam weight × candidate weakness.
(4) Tailor: produce a per-candidate or per-cohort study path emphasising under-covered themes. Humans verify ECO version, sign off content edits, and own pedagogical sequencing.
Business Environment: 8% → 26% (+18%)

rule.statement: Allocate study and content effort proportional to (exam_weight × current_gap), not to weight alone. A domain at 26% weight that is 80% under-covered deserves priority over a 41% domain that is 90% covered.
rule.rationale: Marginal return on study effort is highest where the gap is largest relative to weight. Flat allocation by weight ignores prior coverage.

rule.statement: Pin the ECO version date in every curriculum artifact. ECO updates quarterly; curricula must declare which version they target (e.g., eco_version: 2026-Q3).
rule.rationale: Candidates and organisations using an outdated ECO version prepare for the wrong exam form.

rule.statement: Business Environment content must cover at least 5 of these 7 themes: business case development, benefits realisation, strategic alignment, organisational change, value stream mapping, AI-in-PM, sustainability. Fewer than 5 is a major gap.
rule.rationale: These are the explicit exam themes for the new Business Environment domain; missing any three creates a systematic weakness in the highest-weighted questions.

rule.statement: Do not collapse Process and Business Environment into generic "PM theory". The 2026 exam uses distinct question styles per domain; integrated content produces candidates who cannot distinguish between them.
rule.rationale: Domain-specific question styles require domain-specific preparation; cross-domain blurring reduces exam performance even when total content coverage is high.

rule.statement: Practise tailoring decisions explicitly with scenario-based flashcards: "given context X (team size, complexity, regulation, stakeholder risk), choose predictive / agile / hybrid because Y." Do not memorise methodology names — memorise tailoring rationale.
rule.rationale: Tailoring is the exam's most heavily implied theme across all domains. Candidates who can label frameworks but cannot apply tailoring decisions underperform on scenario questions.

rule.statement: Treat sustainability as a first-class theme covering environmental, social, and economic dimensions. Reference a specific standard (GPM P5 v2.0, ISO 14001, or ISO 21502 sustainability clause) — do not leave it as an abstract goal.
rule.rationale: The exam expects candidates to cite frameworks, not principles. "Be environmentally aware" is not an exam-ready answer; "apply GPM P5 Standard" is.

antipattern.description: Agents citing "PMI Sustainability Standard" as a single document.
antipattern.reason: No such single document exists. The correct references are GPM P5 v2.0, ISO 14001, ISO 21502 sustainability provisions, and UN SDGs. Clarify which standard applies to the context.

rule.statement: Pin the AI-in-PM topic list per ECO version; do not let the agent re-derive it from training data. AI-in-PM surface area is unstable — freeze a topic list per ECO version.
rule.rationale: LLM training data on AI-in-PM is ahead of the ECO content; agents generate plausible but unverified topics that do not map to exam questions.

rule.statement: Distinguish governance (oversight frameworks, decision rights, accountability) from PMO operations and from SOX-style financial controls — the exam tests each separately. Do not conflate them in curriculum content.
rule.rationale: Governance theme overlaps PMO operations and PfMP/PgMP scope and financial controls; agents and curricula routinely conflate them, producing content that prepares for none of them well.

rule.statement: Frame value delivery in terms of outcomes (benefits realised by stakeholders) not outputs (deliverables produced). Exam questions test whether candidates orient around business value, not deliverable completion.
rule.rationale: PMBOK 7 explicitly shifted from output-orientation (knowledge areas, deliverables) to outcome-orientation (performance domains, value). Curricula that still frame success as "on time, on budget, on scope" are misaligned.

antipattern.description: LLMs trained pre-2025 emit the old domain weights (42/50/8) with high confidence.
antipattern.reason: Force the new split (33/41/26) explicitly in the system prompt; do not rely on model knowledge for weight values.

antipattern.description: Crosswalk threshold sensitivity: 0.70 vs 0.75 cosine similarity changes coverage assessment by 20+ percentage points.
antipattern.reason: Pin and document the threshold; output must report which threshold was used alongside coverage percentages.

list.item: eco-fetcher — fetches PMI Exam Content Outline PDF, parses domains/tasks/enablers into JSON.
crosswalk-builder — embeds ECO tasks + content chunks, tags coverage with similarity threshold.
gap-prioritizer — orders gaps by (exam_weight × candidate_weakness × content_coverage_gap).
tailoring-advisor — emits the "tailoring decision tree" recommended by the exam: predictive vs adaptive vs hybrid for given inputs.
sustainability-mapper — maps sustainability theme to a chosen reference standard (ISO 21502 / GPM-P5 / UN SDGs).
eco.json: PMI 2026 ECO parsed
content_corpus: list of {id, title, text, tags, domain}
candidate_profile (optional): {strengths:[domain], weaknesses:[domain]}
Pin ECO version date in output.

For BE domain, require evidence of coverage in: business case, benefits realization, strategic alignment, sustainability, AI-in-PM, governance, value-stream-mapping. If less than 5 of 7 covered, flag major gap.
pdfplumber / pypdf — Parse the PMI ECO PDF
chromadb / qdrant — Embedding store for ECO ↔ content crosswalk
sentence-transformers — Generate embeddings
pandas — Coverage tables, gap reports
pandoc — Render gap reports → PDF
git — Version-control study path + content tags
obsidian-cli — Sync the curriculum into a study vault
PMI ATP content — Authoritative; mostly proprietary
Disciplined Agile Browser — PMI member access, decision tables
PrepCast / PMTraining — Verify post-2026 update date before use
Coursera / LinkedIn Learning — Variable freshness
Anki + AnkiConnect — Spaced repetition; programmable card creation
Obsidian + Spaced Repetition — Curriculum vault
GitBook / Notion — Public-facing curriculum
Brainscape / Quizlet — Card decks

section.name: Coverage Scoring Example

list.item: Pin the ECO version date; ECO updates quarterly and curricula must declare which version they target.
Allocate study/content effort proportional to (exam_weight × current_gap), not to weight alone.
Treat sustainability + AI-in-PM as first-class themes; they sit inside Business Environment and are easy to under-cover.
Practice tailoring decisions explicitly: build flashcards "given context X, choose predictive/agile/hybrid because…".
When updating training content for the 2026 exam, version the docs with a `eco_version: 2026-Q3` front-matter field for traceability.
Do not collapse Process and Business Environment into "PM theory"; the new exam has distinct question styles per domain.
